mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that triggers short-term algorithmic buying, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 1.0 percent and a declining unemployment rate creates a soft-landing environment that maximizes equity risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent confirms economic stagnation, forcing equity markets to discount future earnings sharply and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth drops below the 1.0 percent threshold, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal shows a negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, negative real interest rates trigger inflation hedging and equity deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when inflation expectations remain anchored, or the joint condition yields positive returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
